author: @KerrFamilyLaw
text:
Wow. Things really have changed since I last took physics and “science” when I was 14. Oh please do more ‘fundamentals’ descriptions of how to pigeon-hole concepts! For example - what is going on with neutrons? And is it really true then that a “positive charge” is actually an exact accounting of specific charges like the exact number of protons, each with its own discrete ‘number’ in the positive realm - and that a negative charge can also be measured in exactness but using a much finer scale - being the exact number of electrons?.....so “charge” then would be something that goes up and down (in a system) in a linear way - or along a bi-directional scale, which is directly opposite to each other such that one unit up in charge positive would be equivalent to one unit down in charge negative direction.....BUT something that is not looked at or measured or viewed in terms of ‘charge’ positive or charge negative is what then? Like magnetic poles are not understood in the same universe? A South Pole is not described in terms of a  “negative” and a North Pole is not described in terms of a positive - but in my obviously very rudimentary understanding of matter- it is not clear to me why these are not called positive and negative when it sort of seems like they act in relation to each other as though they “should” be assigned a charge. Somehow what seems to me to be two incommensurable descriptive universes must relate to each other because the forces exerted by magnetic poles (or is it not a “pole” but the whole magnet “system” that influences/induces electron flow in a wire/coil passing through the magnetic “system/field”) are measured by the exact negative “charges” that are directly mappable to the number of electrons.
     How do these north poles and south poles and positive and negative charges relate to each other? I see there can easily be devised a measure of the strength of a magnetic force by definition like one Mforce equals the amount of energy (or attraction or repulsion?) required to move one unit of mass either toward or away from the centre of the attractive or repulsive ‘force’ - but making up units of force so we can do manipulations with matter/stuff/things/ and use numbers to count effects in a systematic way doesn’t get at the “essence” of what these forces ARE... and I suspect you are going to say that there is no “thing” that these forces “ARE”, but that which can be understood by the relations between them - yet it seems like electrons and electricity do actually map onto something graspable and countable and stable - or is this apprehension merely an artifact of mathematics....? We seem so amazed at the constants found in the measurement of the natural world, yet are the mathematical relations we identify simply an artifact of the language of mathematics? In other words, are you saying that the regularity we see in causes and effects and relations between everything-for example temperatures and states and numbers of protons and weights is based in the nature of measurement itself, and we are only grasping/seeing  what we can grasp/see based on the particular tools we use to explore the relations between things? And all of the regularity is an artifact of mathematics, which is fundamentally a system that  is driven by manipulating discrete “units”, and everything is only “regular” because our units of measurement and all regularities therein follow from simple “counting” of objects in our environment? I’m not trying to be all eclectic and theoretical or clever - I’m asking if what you are saying about the pigeon holes is in fact that there is no known underlying explanation for “how things work and what everything is made of” - or if, as a physicist and chemist you actually do grasp the fundamental nature of the physical universe and can explain magnetic phenomenon in the language of electrons and molecules and bonds - and charges that are positive and less positive and negative or less negative on a linear scale and not simply positive and negative in relation to each other, when considering 2 different ‘charge’ states ? Do you understand the fundamental nature of the fundaments of nature? And I have merely to learn more and I will understand it too?
     I hope, (if anyone ever reads this) that I am demonstrating the extent of my ignorance about what seems to be almost everything, so that you can give lectures that can help me move from this perplexing state of ignorance and misunderstanding which I believe I have amply demonstrated, toward an understanding of how everything fits together when an expert understands the world. If you get what I mean. I found this lecture very enlightening and fear I have lost the focus. But what you have said seems to be a view from a distance that I have not encountered before and I would very much enjoy more lectures like this which seem to get at the fundamental nature of simple matter. 
    So if you would like to know what topics are appealing to the interested ignoramus, I would like further explanations about:
1. The bonds you speak of and how you know these things are ‘true’
2. The nature of neutrons
3. What is the North and South Pole and how do these terms relate to positive and negative charges
4. Is there even something that is positive or is it just the absence of negative?
5. Why is it that it takes 4 x as much energy to go from 0 degrees liquid water to 0 degrees frozen water than it does to lower the  temperature of a box of stuff from room temperature to 0 degrees? (from ‘tech ingredients’ Pelletier modules thermo-cooling videos)
6. What is it about “coils” of wire that is important? How is the shape of a conductor determining anything? Is it simply to do with increasing the area, in the way that it seems “universal” that when more of something is needed or wanted or thrust (into something limited or at rest) under pressure in a particular unit of space then flat outside surfaces expand by breaking into smaller flat surfaces (like brains) turning the interior of a material into exterior faces increasing the available area for the exchange of electrons or other little bits of things.../
7. What is so different about the “inside” surfaces vs the “outside” surfaces that makes this interface so important?
Thank you very much indeed for all your work inspiring me to learn and explaining how things work and why they act the way they act and thank you for explaining how to make things that are potentially useful. Totally totally amazing and cool. Wish I was young again so I could spend my life doing hard science instead of soft science. I thought I wasn’t smart enough (I was) to understand math or physics or chemistry or even biology. 
     I firmly believe young people should not be given the choice to “drop” science and math at the age of 13-14 (or financial literacy, or parenting, or comparative religions, or the psychology of aggression or the psychology of love and sex, or the psychology of how humans think and use logic (or don’t) and show bias in understanding, and a host of other topics “future citizens of the world” should understand to be able to best navigate a self-directed life in a community of their choosing: blah blah blah). Cheers.

author: @quintosliber9894
text: If chemistry can be defined as the science of change, and pairing it with the 'land of chem' being very ancient egypt, do you ever read much about the metaphysical pre-alchemical beginnings? Most of the oldest knowledge can be read allegorical to deduce laws of actual matter and theory of the matter. The hydrogen, hydrogen, oxygen bond can be viewed as a 1,1, phi triangle. As above so below can be considered as the first truth. Interested in your thoughts, thanks for all the content, I'm really enjoying it. Cheers.

author: @Doncious
text: do you think you can do a bit on ionic liquids (ILs) or protic ionic liquids (PILs) i think you used those chemicals in your latest copper nano particles vid. My question pertains to synthesizing them what materials to use and how much water can go in them. It seems they are a "wonder" chemical acting as a solvent, surfactant, aids in exfoliation in electrochemical methods and in some work act as direct nitrogen doped precursors for the HTC/graphitization of carbon nano particles. I am wondering if you have come in contact with these liquid salts in in your research. For example it says it is as easy as combining vinegar with an amine. I think this video would be helpful because it would introduce in laymen's terms what these chemicals are and how they can aid in the production of grapheme or other carbon derivatives. Thanks and i hope you consider this a necessary part of your work.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: no worries mate - i will give it a go when i get time

replies.author: @Doncious
replies.text: as it happens robert additional research brought me to something better then pils or ils but natural deep euetic solvents which are greener and cheaper to get a hold of. i found it fascinating that the solids when combined turn liquid!. citric acid and sugar  gets u a solvent as good as anything i suppose. have you run into these class of solvents in your research?
